title: Monotone-constrained booster for wear and urgency variables
summary: Official XGBoost and LightGBM docs note monotonic constraints can improve predictive performance when strong directional priors are valid.
rationale: Official XGBoost and LightGBM docs note monotonic constraints can improve predictive performance when strong directional priors are valid. This task has several near-structural features where more wear or more finish infeasibility should not reduce stop urgency after conditioning on race phase.
implementation_hint: Train a specialist XGBoost/LightGBM with monotone increases on features like `TyreLife_ToFinish_CompoundRatio`, `Compound_LapsBeyondFinishWindow`, `Positive_LapTime_Delta`, `Deg_Per_TyreLife`, and perhaps decreases on `LapsRemaining_Est` in specific formulations. Use only on carefully chosen engineered features, then blend with the unconstrained baseline.
expected_effect: Better out-of-sample ordering under distribution shift by suppressing spurious non-monotone splits in small regimes.
risk: Wrong constraints or over-constraining broad features can flatten useful interactions and reduce peak CV.
